response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and web developer with breadth across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the role demands principal-level hardware performance architecture: SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design. Nothing in the resume demonstrates experience in any of these areas. The closest match is low-latency DLang microservices work and a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments, which falls far short of the required depth in computer architecture and deep-learning performance engineering.
strengths: Solid low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O, caching) at Sociomantic
Broad polyglot engineering background including C, C++, and embedded-adjacent work
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU performance background
